title: Sedriss QL
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}}
{{Youmay|a [[Human/Legends|Human]]|the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] named [[Sedriss]]}}
{{Conflicting|''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]''|[[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'' audio drama]]}}

{{Quote|If you're smart you'll join the Emperor, Skywalker. He can make you as powerful as he's made me!|Sedriss|audio=Sedriss.flac|<ref name="de2 audio">[[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'' audio drama]]</ref>}}
'''Sedriss QL''', [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] '''Qaga Lok''', was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adept]] and [[Executor (Imperial rank)|Military Executor]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]]. As a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] youth, Qaga Lok had found work as a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]], and at some stage he [[Death/Legends|killed]] several [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]]. Lok was arrested and sentenced to death for the crime, but was relieved by members of the Imperial [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitorius]], who brought him before [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] himself. Palpatine was able to convince Lok to swear fealty to him, and so the Emperor began training the mercenary in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. To honor his new Master, Lok took the [[Sith (language)|old Sith]] word "Sedriss" and made it his new name, keeping only the initials of his old one.

When Palpatine died at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the disparate elements of the Galactic Empire [[Imperial Civil War|turned on one another]]. Sedriss, however, and many Dark Side Adepts like him, retreated to the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] world of [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], to await Palpatine's return in a new [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body. Sure enough, the Emperor was [[Transfer essence|reborn]], and to reward Sedriss' loyalty, he made his apprentice Executor of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]], leader of the [[Dark Side Elite]], and arbiter of [[Operation Shadow Hand]], a series of strikes against strategic non-Imperial worlds.

Palpatine died yet again at the [[Battle of Pinnacle Base]] in 10 ABY, but in his absence, Sedriss remained confident that Palpatine would return once more. The Executor eliminated several dark side adepts who attempted to seize power in Palpatine's absence, and eventually, Sedriss' faith was rewarded when the Emperor was reborn in yet another clone body. Palpatine was pleased with the Executor's fidelity, and he tasked Sedriss with the capture of the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. While [[Skirmish on Ossus|attempting]] to capture Skywalker on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], Sedriss was killed by the ancient [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Ood Bnar]], who the Executor in turn managed to kill as his final act.

==Biography==
===Recruitment===
{{Quote|Would you like to kill me?|Palpatine|<ref name="dssb">''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]''</ref>}}
The [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="negtc" /> Qaga Lok,<ref name="ff">{{FactFile|85|SED 1-4|Exekutor Sedress}} German version</ref> [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] sometime after [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]],<ref name="birth date" /> was a distant descendant of the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]], who had fought in the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] [[3997 BBY|thousands of years]] prior.<ref name="ff" /> [[Gender/Legends|He]] became a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and displayed an [[Force-sensitive/Legends|aptitude]] for use of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="dssb" /> At some stage, he worked for [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] as a gun-for-hire.<ref name="jatm" /> Several attempts were made by organizations within the Galactic Empire to recruit him, to little avail. At some stage, Lok [[Death/Legends|killed]] a group of [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]], but was captured and sentenced to death for his crime. Before the sentence could be carried out, members of the Imperial [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitorius]] took custody of him, and brought the young man before [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] himself. The [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] challenged Lok to a duel—if the mercenary won, he would be free to kill Palpatine. If he lost, he would have to swear unwavering fealty to the Emperor, or suffer an excruciatingly painful death.<ref name="dssb" />

[[File:Elite1.jpg|thumb|left|185px|Sedriss, Palpatine, and other members of the Dark Side Elite]]
Lok agreed to the duel—and was instantaneously [[Telekinesis/Legends|crushed]] to the ground through the Force by the Emperor's power. Accepting defeat, the mercenary conceded to honor their agreement. Despite this, Palpatine continued his attack, pressing down on Lok, and cracking the man's ribs. Lok continued to insist that he would serve the Emperor, but Palpatine only relented when the young man begged for his life. When he regained his energy, Lok knelt before the Emperor, acknowledging the more powerful wielder of the Force. Subsequently, Lok underwent extensive training under Palpatine, hoping to wield the same power his Master did.<ref name="dssb" /> To honor his new Master, Lok took on a new name: "Sedriss QL." Retaining only the initials of his old name, he derived the name "Sedriss" from the old [[Sith (language)|Sith language]].<ref name="ff" /> Over the course of his training in the ways of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], Sedriss was never told what his function was to be in the Empire, and the Emperor never discussed the subject.<ref name="dssb" /> In addition to the other skills he acquired during his instruction, Sedriss was also trained to utilize a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].<ref name="de2 1" />

When, in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Palpatine died at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]],<ref name="Endor date">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Death/Legends|death]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].</ref> Sedriss, like most other [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adepts]], retreated to the Emperor's [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Byss/Legends|Byss]]. Sedriss personally oversaw the planetary defense of the world, while the disparate elements of the Empire, lacking a unifying leader, [[Imperial Civil War|turned on one another]]. Despite this, Sedriss realized that the Emperor's [[Clone Labs|clone labs]] in [[Emperor's Citadel|his citadel]] on Byss would have to be kept safe, if Palpatine were to return.<ref name="dssb" /> In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]],<ref name="Emperor return date">''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' states that [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] was [[Transfer essence|reborn]] in a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body five [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after his [[Death/Legends|death]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which is dated to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the Emperor was finally [[Transfer essence|reborn]] in a new [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body, and as a reward for Sedriss' loyalty, he granted his apprentice the rank of [[Executor (Imperial rank)|Executor]],<ref name="dssb" /> which meant that he was in total command of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]].<ref name="de2 1" />

The Emperor also tasked his new Executor with ensuring that no ostensibly loyal Imperial agency seized control of the Empire, should he die again. To this end, he charged Sedriss with the security of his clone labs. Another task the Executor was given in the event of Palpatine's death was the running of [[Operation Shadow Hand]], a series of strikes against non-Imperial worlds using the latest arms and weaponry in an attempt to place those worlds under Imperial rule.<ref name="dssb" /> During this time, Sedriss was also placed in charge of the [[Dark Side Elite]]—a select cadre of seven warriors empowered with the dark side of the Force by Palpatine himself.<ref name="de2 1" /> The Elite were under the total control of Palpatine, although Sedriss possessed slightly more autonomy than the rest.<ref name="negtc" />

===Balmorra===
{{Quote|<s></s>''Beltane''—our troops will land at once to accept your ''surrender''.|Sedriss QL|<ref name="de2 1" />}}
When Palpatine died yet again at the [[Battle of Pinnacle Base]] in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]],<ref name="de 6">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of [[Dark Empire 6|''Dark Empire'' 6]], which features [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Death/Legends|death]] at the [[Battle of Pinnacle Base]], to [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]].</ref> Sedriss, commanding the Dark Side Elite, initiated Operation Shadow Hand, and launched a series of strikes against worlds close to the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. The Executor also stayed loyal to the dead Emperor, and remained faithful that Palpatine would return.<ref name="de2 1" /> In [[Bast Castle]] on the planet [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]], a holding of the late [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], Sedriss had the Dark Side Elite prepare for Palpatine's rebirth.<ref name="negtc" /> While there, Sedriss had a large statue of Vader erected.<ref name="DE2_6">[[Dark Empire II 6|''Dark Empire II'' 6]]</ref> While preparations were underway, one of the Dark Side Elite, [[Kam Solusar]], defected to the enemy [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], following an encounter with the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. Despite the loss, Sedriss remained undaunted. To command his ground forces in Operation Shadow Hand, the Executor appointed [[General/Legends|General]] [[Maximilian Veers/Legends|Maximilian Veers]], a man who he hated, but possessed undeniable battlefield prowess. Despite Veers' expanded duty, Sedriss demoted him to [[Captain/Legends|captain]] for the task.<ref name="negtc" />

One of the strikes encompassed in Operation Shadow Hand garnered Sedriss' personal attention—the disciplining of [[Balmorra/Legends|Balmorra]], a world located on the fringes of the Core Worlds. Balmorra was where the Empire's [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transports]] had been manufactured, and with the news of Palpatine's death at Pinnacle Base, [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Hinch Beltane]], the planet's leader, had begun arming the New Republic. With fellow Dark Side Elite members [[Vill Goir]] and [[Krdys Mordi]] at his side, Sedriss traveled to Balmorra on the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Avenger/Legends|''Avenger'']]. Upon arrival, Goir had the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] and its accompanying [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] hold its fire and instead deploy [[Troop/Legends|troops]] for ground assault.<ref name="de2 1" />

[[File:ImperialHaggling-DEII1.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sedriss contacts Beltane.]]
On the ''Avenger''{{'s}} [[Viewscreen/Legends|viewscreen]], Sedriss was directly contacted by Beltane, who demanded to know what the Executor meant in bringing a fleet to Balmorra. Sedriss, however, saw through Beltane's facade, and gave the governor the opportunity to surrender his operations and be executed for treason. If Beltane would not comply, Sedriss claimed he would attack Balmorra. The governor retorted, claiming that he was still providing the Empire with their most advanced armor. Mordi reminded Sedriss that Balmorra was now producing cutting-edge [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]]—the [[SD-10 battle droid|SD-10]]. The Executor was aware of this, however, and he was confident that the power of the dark side would allow the Imperials to overcome any resistance Beltane could offer. As the Executor landed his troops, he informed Beltane that he would accept the governor's surrender. Beltane refused, as he relished the chance to fight off an Imperial invasion with his new war [[Droid/Legends|droids]].<ref name="de2 1" />

Sedriss had his forces under the command of Captain Veers move into the plains around Balmorra's capital, [[Bin Prime]], in the hopes of luring out the governor's forces. As the Executor predicted, Beltane deployed his SD-10 battle droids. Sedriss' [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] forces were [[Battle of Balmorra|slaughtered]] by the thousands, and his year-old [[SD-9 battle droid]]s were easily outflanked by the newer models. Satisfied that Beltane had deployed his full strength, Sedriss had Mordi give the order for the Imperial troops to pull back. The ''Avenger'' was carrying a complement of a new type of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], the [[Shadow Droid]], devised by one of Palpatine's top engineers, [[Umak Leth]]. Sedriss deployed the fighters, and sent them down to Balmorra, where they decimated the SD-10s. The governor was not beaten yet, though, and he deployed a brand new type of war droid, unknown to the forces of the Empire: the [[X-1 Viper]].<ref name="de2 1" /> The X-1 engaged Sedriss' Shadow Droids, and Captain Veers was killed in the ensuing combat.<ref name="de2 audio" />

Sedriss was surprised at the turn of events, and demanded that he be put back in contact with Beltane. Although the Executor could have turned his fleet's guns on Balmorra, he chose to admit that Beltane had won the battle on the planet surface, and inquired as to what the governor wanted in exchange for the X-1. Beltane wanted Balmorra's freedom, and claimed that the Galactic Empire could simply buy the munitions from the planet on a contract basis, provided that the governor agree to the contract. Sedriss was outraged, claiming that the Emperor did not buy weapons from free planets, but Beltane retorted, reminding the Executor that Palpatine was dead. Conceding the point, Sedriss prepared to send down representatives to the planet to negotiate terms. Goir and Mordi wanted to have the planet leveled, but Sedriss refused, as he needed Beltane's weapons as soon as possible. He explained, though, that once he had the war droids and the men who designed them, he would destroy Balmorra.<ref name="de2 1" />

===Return of the Emperor===
{{Quote|<s></s>''My Master!''|Sedriss QL|audio=Sedriss-MyMaster.flac|<ref name="de2 audio" />}}
[[File:Elite2.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Sedriss and the Dark Side Elite arrive at the Emperor's Citadel.]]
Meanwhile, back at the Emperor's Citadel on Byss, rumors had begun to circulate amongst the Dark Side Adepts that Palpatine would not take on another clone body. The same adepts that had spread the rumors suggested that Sedriss should be executed for his failure to subjugate Balmorra. Sedriss and four other members of the Dark Side Elite raced back to Byss in their [[I-7 Howlrunner]]. Landing at the Emperor's Citadel, Sedriss entered the Emperor's cloning laboratory, to find most of Palpatine's clone bodies destroyed. Present were the perpetrators—adepts [[Nefta]] and [[Sa-Di]]. Nefta immediately confronted Sedriss about his failure at Balmorra, and the two adepts attempted to justify their destruction of the clone bodies. The Executor, however, would hear none of it, and quickly [[Skirmish on Byss|engaged Nefta in a duel]]. In no time, he swiftly struck the adept down, before slaying Sa-Di.<ref name="de2 1" />

At that moment, a lone figure entered the doorway to the lab—Emperor Palpatine, returned in a younger clone body once more. Upon seeing his reborn Master, Sedriss fell to his knee, and told the Emperor that he had eliminated the last of the unfaithful adepts. Palpatine was satisfied, but did not waste time—he ordered Sedriss to bring him the pregnant [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] before she gave birth to her [[Anakin Solo|third child]],<ref name="de2 1" /> as he hoped to possess the child should his current new clone body fail him.<ref name="ee">[[Empire's End 2|''Empire's End'' 2]]</ref> He also ordered Sedriss to array the entire Imperial military against the Jedi Knight Luke Skywalker, Organa Solo's brother, who had been a unrelenting and persistent enemy of Palpatine for many years. The Emperor told the Executor that he would deal with the armed forces of the New Republic himself.<ref name="de2 1" />

Sedriss decided to [[Mission to Nar Shaddaa (Operation Shadow Hand)|send]] Dark Side Elite members [[Zasm Katth]] and [[Baddon Fass]] to capture Organa Solo, but they were killed while attempting to do so, and the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Invincible (Imperial-class)|''Invincible'']] was lost.<ref name="de2 2">[[Dark Empire II 2|''Dark Empire II'' 2]]</ref> Palpatine was less than pleased, and suggested to Sedriss that he should be executed for his failure. Sedriss quickly dismissed the issue of Katth and Fass, claiming that he had located Skywalker and the Jedi's companion, former Dark Side Elite member Kam Solusar; one of his [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droids]] had tracked the two to the world of [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]],<ref name="de2 3">[[Dark Empire II 3|''Dark Empire II'' 3]]</ref> that had served as a headquarters for the Jedi [[4000 BBY/Legends|thousands of years prior]].<ref name="FNU">''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising]]''</ref> Without any New Republic forces to back him up, Sedriss reasoned, Skywalker would be easier prey. Palpatine conjectured that Skywalker was there to investigate the past of the Jedi, in an attempt to revive them to their former strength. Realizing that Skywalker could not be permitted to do so, he insisted that the Jedi be stopped, but Sedriss was already prepared—the probe droid was authorized to kill Skywalker and Solusar as soon as they arrived on Ossus. Palpatine, however, vehemently opposed such a course of action, and ordered his Executor to travel to Ossus and personally capture Skywalker as well as the traitor. The Emperor promised the Executor a great deal of power if he succeeded, but only "madness beyond death" should he fail.<ref name="de2 3" />

===Death on Ossus===
{{Quote|<s></s>''Curse'' you, Jedi. I don't care if you're a ''million'' years old—No one imprisons a Dark Jedi!|Sedriss|audio=Sedriss-CurseYou.flac|<ref name="de2 audio" />}}
Sedriss gave the order for the probe droid to stand down, but the message was lost in [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] static. The droid engaged Skywalker and Solusar, but they were able to destroy it without being harmed themselves.<ref name="de2 3" /> Meanwhile, the Executor researched Ossus, specifically the legends of the Jedi who had survived its [[Cron Supernova/Legends|devastation]] thousands of years prior. In his research, however, Sedriss learned of the primitive [[Ysanna/Legends|Ysanna]] tribesmen and decided he could enlist them as his servants.<ref name="potj">''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]''</ref> The Executor then made his way to Ossus aboard the [[Imperial hunter (starship)|Imperial hunter]] ''[[Scourge One]]''.<ref name="de2 3" />

Skywalker and Solusar were located near some old Jedi ruins, in the company of several Ysanna tribesmen. [[Darktrooper]] squads were deployed to engage the Ysanna, and Sedriss hoped they would clear the way to an engagement with Skywalker and Solusar. He and Goir, who had accompanied him, watched on as the Jedi moved to [[Skirmish on Ossus|engage]] the darktroopers. Within twenty minutes, the Imperial troopers had all been killed.<ref name="de2 3" />

Sedriss observed that Skywalker had been weakened by the battle, however, and he moved in with Goir to attack the Jedi. The pair of them utilized Force choke against their adversaries, and while Solusar felt the effects of the attack, Skywalker was able to block it. Angered by Skywalker's taunts, Sedriss ordered Goir to [[Cho sun/Legends|cleave both Jedi's fighting arms]], as they were to be taken alive. Lightsabers at the ready, the two Dark Side Adepts engaged the two Jedi. Goir was [[Sai tok/Legends|sliced in two]] by Solusar, and Sedriss was sent hurtling back by a Force push from Skywalker.<ref name="de2 3" />

[[File:OodBnarSacrifice-DEII3.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sedriss and Bnar eliminate one another.]]
Disarmed, and with his back against a tree, Sedriss took one of the [[Gender/Legends|female]] Ysanna natives, [[Jem Ysanna|Jem]], hostage. He threatened to destroy Jem with the power of the dark side, but the native tried to use the Force against him. Surmising that Skywalker hoped to take the Force-adept Ysanna and train them as Jedi, the Executor declared that he would take Jem back to Byss and turn her to the dark side. He also stated that he would bring Solusar back with him, but the turncoat Dark Side Elite member adamantly refused.<ref name="de2 3" />

At that moment, the tree—actually the [[Neti/Legends|Neti]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Ood Bnar]],<ref name="de2 3" /> who had been in a form of hibernation since the Great Sith War<ref name="tsw">''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War]]''</ref>—reawakened, and used his tendrils to grasp and hold Sedriss. Sedriss sensed that he was now engaged in his final battle,<ref name="de2 3" /> but he did not intend to let Skywalker escape.<ref name="de2 audio" /> Using all his power, he called down energy from the planet's charged [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], and used it in droves of [[Force lightning/Legends|lightning]] to attack Bnar. The Executor still held on to Jem, but Skywalker was able to pull her free just as Bnar called upon the energy of the planet beneath him to obliterate both Sedriss and himself.<ref name="de2 3" /> In the wake of Sedriss' death on Ossus, the Emperor appointed [[Xecr Nist]] as the new Executor.<ref name="de2 4">[[Dark Empire II 4|''Dark Empire II'' 4]]</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|One of the subtleties of military art is using your ''enemy's resources'' to defeat him.|Sedriss QL|<ref name="de2 1" />}}
[[File:Sedriss-NEGTC.png|thumb|left|150px|Sedriss]]
As a young male, Qaga Lok was found by Imperial authorities to be insolent, insubordinate, and overtly violent. Upon meeting him, Emperor Palpatine deduced that he could not respect anyone he could easily kill. When Lok was defeated by Palpatine, he pledged his unfaltering loyalty to the Emperor.<ref name="dssb" /> He respected his Master enough to change his name to "Sedriss QL," in honor of Palpatine.<ref name="ff" /> Sedriss was devoted to his liege, and would instantaneously drop to his knee upon sight of the Emperor.<ref name="de2 1" /> Palpatine was not always pleased with Sedriss' performance as Executor, but Sedriss was able to save himself by promising the Emperor results.<ref name="de2 3" /> Even when out of the Emperor's presence, Sedriss displayed a great amount of fealty to Palpatine, vowing to avenge himself on those responsible for the Emperor's numerous deaths. Sedriss also spoke highly of Palpatine's ability to grant considerable power to others.<ref name="de2 1" />

Sedriss was pragmatic enough to not resort to total destruction of a valuable world such as Balmorra—although he disliked Governor Beltane, he realized that it would not be practical to have the planet [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombarded]], as many useful and valuable factories would be obliterated. Although Sedriss was irate at Beltane's victory in their initial engagement, the Executor saw men like the governor to be insignificant, compared to adversaries such as Luke Skywalker. Sedriss commanded what he thought to be considerable foresight as a military commander, and he was willing to sacrifice the lives of his men if it meant he would capture a valuable world such as Balmorra with its facilities intact. When faced with an eventuality he did not prepare for, though, Sedriss did not retaliate in rage, and instead chose to negotiate with Governor Beltane.<ref name="de2 1" />

The Executor was confident that the power of the dark side of the Force would aid him in battle, and that it would overcome the state-of-the-art weaponry he and his fellow Dark Side Elite encountered. He also credited the dark side with his superior combat abilities when dueling the dark side adept Nefta. When in battle, Sedriss would not hesitate to strike down an opponent, even if they were not prepared to defend themselves.<ref name="de2 1" /> When defeated by Skywalker, Sedriss became desperate, using an innocent bystander as a hostage. Even as he was trapped by the ancient Jedi Ood Bnar, however, he would not surrender.<ref name="de2 3" /> If he was to die, he would try to take Skywalker and the other Jedi with him.<ref name="de2 audio" /> Sedriss had [[Color/Legends|black]] hair and brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]].<ref name="negtc" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|What's the matter, Nefta—is your ''magic'' of no use to you against a ''dark side warrior?''|Sedriss|<ref name="de2 1" />}}
[[File:Sedriss_QL_JATM.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Sedriss in action]]
After Darth Vader, Executor Sedriss was considered to be one of the strongest of Emperor Palpatine's students.<ref name="hde">[[Star Wars Handbook 3: Dark Empire|''Star Wars Handbook'' 3: ''Dark Empire'']]</ref> In spite of this, Palpatine regarded Sedriss as only moderate in his ability to wield to the Force, and decided not to train the Executor as a [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]].<ref name="jvs">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> Sedriss was skilled in the use of a lightsaber, as he quickly out-fenced Nefta, and deftly killed Sa-Di shortly thereafter.<ref name="de2 1" /> He was also able to utilize Force choke, but his powers were effortlessly deflected by the more powerful Jedi, Luke Skywalker. The Jedi bested Sedriss in combat, utilizing Force push against the Executor effectively. In his final hour, Sedriss hurled his mind deep into the depths of the dark side, conjuring up Force lightning in the process, and was able to call upon the energy of Ossus' charged atmosphere to destroy Ood Bnar.<ref name="de2 3" /> Sedriss was able to [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] an I-7 Howlrunner starfighter.<ref name="de2 1" />

==Equipment==
While serving as the Military Executor and the leader of the Dark Side Elite, Sedriss wore a set of garments consisting of a gray shirt and loose black pants supported by an ornate brown-and-silver belt with a large round buckle and an additional strap wrapping around his groin area. The outfift was completed with heavy black boots and armguards, as well as a short black jacket with a high raised collar.<ref name="negtc" /> He was armed with a red-bladed lightsaber.<ref name="hde" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:SedrissQagaLok-GermanFF.jpg|thumb|left|Sedriss's birth name was first revealed in the German edition of ''The Official Star Wars Fact File''.]]
The character of Sedriss made his first appearance in [[Dark Empire II 1|''Dark Empire II'' 1]], the first issue of the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]'' series, written by [[Tom Veitch]], illustrated [[Cam Kennedy]], and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="de2 1" /> on [[December 20]], [[1994]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54063/star_wars_dark_empire_ii_1994_1|text=Star Wars: Dark Empire II (1994) #1}}</ref> The character was later portrayed by [[Jim Ward (voice actor)|Jim Ward]] in the [[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|audio drama adaptation]] of ''Dark Empire II''.<ref name="DE CE">''[[Dark Empire: The Collector's Edition]]''</ref>

Sedriss was originally slated to be introduced in Veitch's proposed short novel [[Lightsider (story)|''Lightsider'']], which would have served as a lead-in into the events of ''Dark Empire II'', where he would have been given the birth name "Qaga Lok." However, ''Lightsider'' was canceled.<ref name="ComicScan">{{CollectorCite|4|ComicScan|ComicScan: Comics That Almost Were}}</ref> Instead, the birth name "Qaga Lok" was later used in the [[Wikipedia:German language|German version]] of the [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 85|85th issue]] of ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]''.<ref name="ff" /> This detail did not feature in the [[Wikipedia:English language|English]] version of issue 85. The name "Sedriss QL" had previously appeared in a special-edition version of issue #1 of ''Dark Empire II'', published by Hero magazine.<ref name="de2 1" />

===Audio drama inconsistencies===
The audio drama of ''Dark Empire II'' differs from the original comic ''Dark Empire II'' in several aspects apart from general dialog changes, in regards to Sedriss' story. During the Battle of Balmorra, Beltane has his planetary defenses fire on Sedriss' fleet, which is what prompts the Executor to land ground troops. Later, when Palpatine returns, Sedriss only encounters and battles Sa-Di, and does not see that the Emperor's clones have been destroyed firsthand. Another minor change occurs during the battle on Ossus, wherein Sedriss is bested by Skywalker in the lightsaber duel, rather than being pushed back up against Ood Bnar by the Force. These presentations of events may be [[canon]] within the continuity of [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]], but for the benefit of the article, they are represented in the biography as told in the comic.<ref name="de2 audio" />
